name: CHOROID PLEXUS CYST
definition: cyst arising from folding of neuroepithelium with trapping of secretory products +

demographics.Incidence: 0.9-3.6% in sonographic population; in up to 50% of autopsied brains
demographics.Histo: epithelial-lined cyst, filled with clear fluid ± debris

dominant_finding: iso- to slightly hyperattenuating compared with CSF
discriminating_features: iso- to slightly hyperattenuating compared with CSF
iso- to hyperintense on T1WI compared with CSF
hyperintense on T2WI compared with CSF

CT: iso- to slightly hyperattenuating compared with CSF
MR: iso- to hyperintense on T1WI compared with CSF
hyperintense on T2WI compared with CSF

clinical.Prognosis: 90% disappear by 26th-28th week; may persist; in 95% of no significance
